strategy: Storm boss with only 695 health, bring cheap, low-pip attacks and AoE.

Never attack Yoshinobu with a 6-pip-or-higher spell (Colossus and the like). It triggers a Chain Reaction: Smoke Screen on you, a 350-heal to all Samoorai, and a Meteor Strike. Keep attacks under 6 pips.
Use a low-pip AoE to hit all the Samoorai at once so nothing survives to heal or retaliate.
Expect Yoshinobu's opening Mass Infection (cuts your healing) and Hisamoto's Legion Shield every 3 rounds, chip through with cheap hits.
If you break the chain rules after Hisamoto is down you will eat Blizzards and Hex/Weakness, so just clear the board fast with sub-6-pip AoE.
cheatNotes: Yoshinobu opens with Mass Infection, cutting your healing.
Hisamoto casts Legion Shield on Round 1 and every 3 rounds after, group shields to work through.
Attack Yoshinobu with a 6+ pip spell and you trigger the Chain Reaction below, keep attacks under 6 pips.
Chain Reaction: Yoshinobu casts a -60% Smoke Screen on you.
Chain Reaction: Hisamoto casts Unicorn, healing all Samoorai 350, why you avoid big single hits.
Chain Reaction: Kensaburo casts Meteor Strike for 355-395 Fire.
After Hisamoto is dead, a 6+ pip attack on Yoshinobu triggers a different chain.
Kensaburo heals each Samoorai 100 with Minor Blessing.
Yoshinobu puts a +25% Hex and -30% Weakness on all wizards.
Yoshinobu casts Blizzard (250-290 Ice) and Sandstorm (255-295 Balance).
